purpose: Declares the CURRENT phase and its slices as DATA, so the owner milestone-update generator (scripts/new-milestone-update.ps1) can compute a REAL '% complete' = done slices / total, never an invented number. A slice counts as done ONLY if it carries non-empty 'evidence' (a PR number, commit SHA, or doc pointer) — flipping 'done' without a checkable artifact is a defect the generator refuses (reports UNKNOWN). Owner-legible on purpose: this is the phase map. Update it as slices land; keep 'evidence' pointing at something real.

phase.name: Operational Trust Qualification & Adoption
phase.tagline: the final phase — prove PCC well enough that the owner signs off he trusts it

slice_note: Slices = the 22 audit categories named explicitly in ADR-0009 (an audit category is 'done' when its per-practice grid is populated AND independently verified), plus the two sign-off gates. The category list is the current declared plan; some may consolidate (ADR-0009 point 1) — adjust here when they do, so the denominator always reflects the real plan.

slices.title: State & data integrity
slices.evidence: audit grid docs/audit/state-data-integrity.md (Codex-verified); 2 real T0 test-gaps CLOSED — backup.spec.js (7) + check-schemas.spec.js (4); failure-injection-in-CI disclosed as accepted residue

slices.title: Verification & proof-of-done
slices.evidence: audit grid docs/audit/verification-proof-of-done.md (Codex-verified); mostly tested-holds (earned by real past failures); built the ONE bar-clearing control: scripts/check-branch-protection.ps1 (+7 tests) converting O1 from owner-assertion to a live-checked fact (ran live → PASS); fixed a stale test-count in PROJECT.md

slices.title: Communication contracts
slices.evidence: audit complete + Codex-verified; channels 1/3/4 machinery built (PR #16, #18) + parity-proven (scaffold-kit.spec.js); 2/5/6/7 assessed with disclosed gaps (channel 6 measurement deferred) — see docs/specs/communication-contracts.md grid

slices.title: Live end-to-end adversarial build + adoption (gate b)
